<!-- SYNCED FROM mate@v0.98.0 · mode:sync · DO NOT EDIT — change upstream then `mate pull` -->
# The report's HTML components — the markup cookbook

These are the shapes `.mate/design/report.css` styles. **Use these class names exactly.**
The aggregate page (`index.html`, generated by `mate report build`) emits the same
vocabulary, so a hand-composed day and a machine-built history look like one product —
they share no DOM, only these names, and that agreement is the whole contract.

Everything else — the hero, the chips, the `route` dividers, the eyebrow, the callout,
the honesty `note`, the footer — belongs to `presentation.css` and the `presentation`
skill. Do not redefine them here.

## KPI row — the numbers, readable at a glance

```html
<ul class="kpis">
  <li class="kpi">
    <span class="kpi-value">34</span>
    <span class="kpi-label">code commits</span>
    <span class="kpi-sub">of 79 total</span>
  </li>
  <li class="kpi">
    <span class="kpi-value">+29 356 / −1 698</span>
    <span class="kpi-label">lines</span>
    <span class="kpi-sub">210 files</span>
  </li>
  <li class="kpi">
    <span class="kpi-value">11.5</span>
    <span class="kpi-label">hours</span>
    <span class="kpi-sub">4 sessions</span>
  </li>
  <li class="kpi">
    <span class="kpi-value">2.1</span>
    <span class="kpi-label">hours on tests</span>
    <span class="kpi-sub">18 test files</span>
  </li>
</ul>
```

## The barline — every epic, including the ones that have not started

An unstarted epic still gets a row, with a plain-language reason. A missing row is a hole
the reader fills with suspicion; a 0% row that says *what it waits on* is information.

```html
<ul class="bars">
  <li class="bar">
    <p class="bar-head">
      <span class="bar-name">Visa launch</span>
      <span class="bar-value">41% <span class="dim">· 7/17 phases</span></span>
    </p>
    <span class="bar-track"><span class="bar-fill" style="width:41%"></span></span>
    <p class="bar-note">Четыре фазы можно начать прямо сейчас; остаётся две волны зависимостей. Оценка: 2–3 дня.</p>
  </li>

  <li class="bar">
    <p class="bar-head">
      <span class="bar-name">Theming</span>
      <span class="bar-value">0% <span class="dim">· 0/9 phases</span></span>
    </p>
    <span class="bar-track"><span class="bar-fill blocked" style="width:0%"></span></span>
    <p class="bar-note">Ещё не начат: ждёт завершения фазы T0 в соседнем эпике.</p>
  </li>

  <li class="bar">
    <p class="bar-head">
      <span class="bar-name">Link health</span>
      <span class="bar-value">100% <span class="dim">· 4/4 phases</span></span>
    </p>
    <span class="bar-track"><span class="bar-fill done" style="width:100%"></span></span>
    <p class="bar-note">Все фазы закрыты.</p>
  </li>
</ul>
```

`.bar-fill` takes one optional modifier: `.done` (green) when every phase is closed,
`.blocked` (amber) when the epic cannot start. Neither is decoration — each says
something the percentage alone does not.

## Shipped — a green check, and *why it matters*

The check is the point of the section, and the `.why` line is the point of the check. A
list of feature names tells the reader nothing they can act on.

```html
<ul class="checks">
  <li>
    <strong>Справочник направлений и типов виз.</strong>
    <span class="why">Каталог перестал быть захардкоженным списком — новую страну добавляет контент-редактор, без релиза.</span>
  </li>
</ul>
```

## Commits — the evidence, in small print

Two lists, and the second is always last on the page.

```html
<h3>Коммиты эпика</h3>
<ul class="commits">
  <li><code>90b0a899</code> feat(destinations): add destination and visa-type entities
      <span class="tag">getvisa-launch-2026-07</span></li>
</ul>

<h3>Обслуживание харнесса</h3>
<ul class="commits small">
  <li><code>c77ee1a4</code> chore(mate): pull v0.80.0</li>
</ul>
```

## The one honesty note

The standard allows exactly one per document, and in a report its occupant is obvious:
the method. Take the sentence from `facts.json` (`method.hours_note`) — do not paraphrase
it into something softer.

```html
<p class="note"><strong>Как посчитаны часы.</strong> Часы выведены из таймстемпов
коммитов: серия коммитов с промежутком меньше 90 минут — одна сессия. Это оценка усилий,
а не измерение времени.</p>
```
